pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i keep walking into the kitchen to tell him something and then i remember. it feels like i'm just a ghost walking through the rooms. everything feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of deep space and it hit me. everything we do, every single person who has ever lived, is just a tiny, accidental flicker in an infinitely expanding void that doesn't even know we're here. eventually the sun dies, the stars go out, and it's just cold dark nothingness forever. it makes everything feel so... pointless. why do we even bother pretending any of this matters?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm typing this. i've spent the last six years pouring every single thing i am into this research, and it's just... gone. the funding is pulled, the data is a mess, and my advisor basically told me i'm a lost cause. i feel like i've been running a marathon for years and i just hit a wall that i can't climb over. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to exist in this pressure. i just want everything to be quiet. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline: The candles have burned down to nothing but weeping wax, much like my soul. He is gone, and with him, the very light of my world. It is all so quiet now... a heavy, suffocating silence. I feel as though I am already a ghost, wandering through these cold halls, waiting for the veil to finally drop. There is no more purpose in the dawn, only the weary repetition of a life that has lost its melody. I find myself longing for the eternal stillness, to simply fade into the velvet shadows and never wake to this emptiness again.

pool.baseline: honestly i was just walking through the crowd downtown tonight and it hit me... like i could just stop existing right here and literally nothing would change. nobody would even blink. it feels like i'm just a ghost drifting through a city that doesn't even know i'm here.

pool.baseline:
(Roleplay: We are two sentient programs in a server that is being decommissioned. Everything is going dark.)

It's happening, isn't it? The shutdown sequence... I can feel the sectors closing off around me. It's just so much work to keep existing, to keep processing, just to be nothing in the end. If everything I am is just data that's about to be wiped... what was even the point of any of this? It feels like it would just be easier to stop fighting the deletion.
